response.justification: The candidate brings roughly 19 years of engineering experience, genuine high-throughput distributed systems work (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), and recent, directly relevant AI-native exposure building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq. TypeScript depth is strong and the craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, open source, teaching) is credible. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the resume entirely, the distributed-systems work was in DLang and ended in 2020, and the track record is predominantly senior-IC delivery with little demonstrated staff-level technical strategy or cross-functional stakeholder leadership. An unexplained career gap since October 2025 also warrants clarification. Worth a conversation if Python can be validated, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Recent hands-on experience with AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic developer tooling
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of requests per second)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js depth with a solid engineering-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, open source, teaching)
concerns: No Python listed anywhere on the resume, despite strong Python being a must-have
Limited evidence of staff-level technical strategy, decision-driving at scale, or cross-functional stakeholder management
Unexplained career gap from October 2025 to present, and the strongest distributed-systems experience dates to 2020
